developers-getting-started--docs.name: Docs
developers-getting-started--docs.title: Developers/Getting started
developers-getting-started--docs.content: import { Meta, Unstyled } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks"; import { tokens } from "@styles/tokens.css.ts"; # Getting Started SPPackel is a collection of React components and styles that can be used to build applications at SPP. You can find the documentation for the components and styles in the sidebar to your left. You will find the Github repository for the project here. ### Prerequisites SPPackel is built using React and requires React and React DOM to be installed in your project. - React - ^18 || ^19 - React DOM - ^18 || ^19 SPPackel only lists them as peer dependencies, to avoid version conflicts. ### Usage To use SPPackel in your project, you need to install the package and import the components you need. #### New project? Use react-starter template. If you are starting a new project, the starter is a great starting point. It will set up a new React project with SPPackel and all the necessary configurations. Use it as a template for your new repository. react-starter repository at Github #### Installation into an existing project SPPackel is distributed as a private npm package. To install it, you need to have access to the private npm registry. You can get access by following the steps below: ##### 1. Generate a GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) You can do that from here. This will act as a password for the GitHub package registry. When you are creating the token, under scopes be sure to check at least repo and read:packages. Make sure to authorize SSO for `spp-technology` on this token by clicking on button/dropdown configure SSO. ##### 2. Login to the npm registry Run ```bash npm login --scope=@spp-technology --registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com ``` - Username: GITHUB_USERNAME - Password: TOKEN_FROM_FIRST_STEP - Email: GITHUB_EMAIL ##### 3. Install the package You can now install SPPackel by running the following command: ```bash npm install @spp-technology/sppackel ``` ##### 4. Import the components You can now import the components in your project like so: ```javascript import { Button } from "@spp-technology/sppackel"; ``` ##### 5. Use the components You can now use the components as you would any other React component: ```jsx ``` ##### 6. Configure SPPackel for your application SPPackel uses a `ConfigurationProvider` to make sure the components suits your application. You can find more information on how to set up this in the sidebar under `Configuration`. ##### 7. Installing SPPackel using Github Actions: To install SPPackel using Github Actions, you will need to update your workflow file with a token to acceess the private npm registry. You can do this by adding the following to your workflow file: ```yaml - name: Install npm packages run: npm ci env: NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_PACKAGES_READ }} ``` An npm access token has been added to the Github "global" secrets for you to use. You can access it by using `${{ secrets.NPM_PACKAGES_READ }}`.

components-alert-guidelines-usage--docs.name: Docs
components-alert-guidelines-usage--docs.title: Components/Alert/Guidelines/Usage
components-alert-guidelines-usage--docs.content:
import { Meta, Unstyled } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks"; # Alert ## Introduction The Alert component is used to communicate important messages to the user. It supports multiple severity levels, each with a reserved color that must not be used for other purposes. ## Headlines Alerts **must** have a headline. If the headline is visually unwanted, there must be another headline for this alert message in the same section in another component. ## Variants & When to Use
Variant Criticality Use when…
error High The user is completely stopped and cannot proceed.
warning Medium It is possible to use certain parts, but attention is needed.
information Low We need to inform the user about something; everything is still working.
success Success An action completed successfully.
superVisible Extra emphasis A regular "message" or "subdued" variant does not stand out enough. Do not overuse. Works well in situations where Konsumentverket is reviewing. Can be placed under H1 + introductory text + documents on fund pages (open pages).
message General General-purpose message without specific severity.
subdued General Lower visual weight message.
## Color Usage The colors assigned to each variant are **reserved** — they may not be used for other purposes outside of alerts.

points list with items", storyPath: "components-usplist" } ]; # Component Overview This page provides a comprehensive list of all available components in SPPackel, our design system component library. {components.map((component) => ( ))}
Component Category Description Status
{component.storyPath ? {component.name} : component.name} {component.figmaUrl && ( )} {component.category} {component.description} {component.status || "N/A"}
## Component Categories The components are organized into the following categories: - **Actions**: Interactive components that trigger actions (Button, IconButton, MainButton) - **Branding**: Brand-related components (Logo) - **Content**: Content display and layout components (ArticleBlock, Banner, ContentBlock, Hero, etc.) - **Data Display**: Components for structured data (LightTable) - **Data Visualization**: Charts and graphs (BarChart, LineChart) - **Development**: Development tools (DevelopmentLabel) - **Display**: General display components (Chip, DataCard, Icon, Label, etc.) - **Feedback**: User feedback components (Alert, Loading, Skeleton) - **Forms**: Form input components (Checkbox, InputDate, InputNumber, InputText, RadioButton, SelectBox, Slider, ToggleSwitch, QuickFilter) - **Layout**: Structural layout components (Block, Box, Container, Footer, Main, PageTop) - **Navigation**: Navigation components (Breadcrumbs, Menu, NavigationButton, Pagination, Stepper, StickyNav, StickyTab, Tab, TabSlider) - **Overlay**: Modal and overlay components (ConfirmDialog, Drawer, Modal, Tooltip) - **Typography**: Text components (BalancedHeader) ## Usage To use any component, import it from `@spp-technology/sppackel`: ```typescript import { Button, Label, Modal } from "@spp-technology/sppackel"; ``` For detailed documentation, examples, and API reference for each component, navigate to the component's individual documentation page in Storybook.
